vocab.ex: Ich esse einen Apfel zum Frühstück.
vocab.note: 'der' = masculine. Accusative: einen Apfel (der → einen).
'das' = neuter. Brot+Zeit = Brotzeit (snack time)!
Uncountable noun — no plural like English.
'ä' sounds like 'e' in bed.
Irregular plural: Ei → Eier.

vocab.ex: Die Tomate ist rot und saftig.
vocab.note: Foreign words ending in -e are often feminine.
Also called 'die Möhre' in Northern Germany!
Germans love potatoes — 100+ potato dishes!
'Kaffee und Kuchen' on Sundays is a German tradition!
'W' in German sounds like English 'V'!
'schmecken' = to taste. 'süß' = sweet.
Milka, Ritter Sport = famous German chocolate brands!
'Möchten' = would like. Very polite form!
'mehr' = more. 'brauchen' = to need.
'aufs' = auf das (on the). Common contraction!
German dogs say 'Wau Wau' not 'Woof'!
German cats say 'Miau'. 'schläft' = sleeps (irregular).
Umlaut plural: Vogel → Vögel. Very common!
'schnell' = fast. Very useful adjective!
Umlaut plural: Kuh → Kühe. German cows say 'Muh'!
'schwimmen' = to swim. 'im' = in dem (contraction).
'stark' = strong. Used literally and figuratively.
Berlin's symbol is a bear (Bär)!
'Mein' = my (masculine). Mein Vater, meine Mutter.
'meine' = my (feminine). Umlaut plural!

vocab.ex: Mein Bruder ist älter als ich.
vocab.note: 'älter als' = older than. Comparative!
'gern' = gladly. 'Sie tanzt gern' = she likes dancing.
Informally: 'der Opa'. Both are commonly used!
Informally: 'die Oma'. Very affectionate!
After verb: 'rot'. Before noun: 'ein rotes Auto' (ending changes)!
'blau sein' also means 'to be drunk' colloquially!
German mailboxes are yellow (Deutsche Post)!
'die Grünen' = the Green political party!
'Schwarzfahren' = riding transit without a ticket!
'ß' (Eszett) sounds like 's', replaces 'ss' after long vowels.
'die Wohnung' = apartment. Most Germans live in apartments!
Imperative: schließen → schließ! (informal command).
Same form in singular and plural!
'im' = in dem. 'schlafen' is irregular: er schläft.
'in der' = location (dative). 'in die' = movement!
Irregular plural with umlaut: Buch → Bücher.
Blei=lead + Stift=pen. German compound word!
'schwer' = heavy AND difficult. Great dual meaning!
'zur' = zu der (to the). 'gehen zur Schule' = go to school.
'scheinen' = to shine AND to seem. Great double meaning!
Weather ALWAYS uses 'es': Es regnet, es schneit, es ist kalt.
'Es schneit' = It is snowing. 'schneien' = to snow.
Umlaut plural: Baum → Bäume. 'Weihnachtsbaum' = Christmas tree!
Neuter gender. 'braune' = brown (adjective ending for plural).
'Ohrwurm' = earworm (a song stuck in your head)!
'Hände schütteln' = to shake hands. Umlaut plural!
'Kopfschmerzen' = headache. Body part + Schmerzen = pain!
English loanword! 'tragen' = to wear. Very common!
'passen' = to fit. 'mir' = me (dative). 'Es passt mir' = It fits me.
'bequem' = comfortable. 'diese' = these (demonstrative).

vocab.ex: Im Winter trage ich einen Mantel.
vocab.note: 'im Winter' = in winter. Always use article with seasons!
'einen warmen' = accusative masculine adjective ending.
'bügeln' = to iron. 'gebügelt' = ironed (past participle).
'Autobahn' = motorway, famous for no speed limit!
'mit dem Bus' = by bus. Mit + dative = means of transport!
'pünktlich' = punctual/on time. Germans value this a lot!
U = Untergrund (underground). S-Bahn = city train!

vocab.ex: Das Krankenhaus ist in der Stadtmitte.
vocab.note: krank=sick + Haus=house. German compound word!
Female: 'die Ärztin'. Add -in for female professions!
Verb and noun share root! kochen (cook) → der Koch (chef).
'helfen' = to help + dative. 'hilft uns' = helps us.
Germany won the World Cup 4 times. National obsession!
'hören' = to listen. 'gerne' = gladly (= likes to).
Strong verb, uses 'sein' in perfect. 'Im Sommer' = in summer.
'kein' = no/not a. 'dabei' = on me/with me.
'zu hoch' = too high. 'zu' + adjective = too + adjective!
'Die Rechnung bitte!' = The bill please! Key restaurant phrase.
French loanword, pronounced French-style!
Germany is world-famous for beer! 'Prost!' = Cheers!
Speise=meal + Karte=card. Also called 'die Karte' informally.

vocab.ex: Mein Ziel ist es, Deutsch zu sprechen.
vocab.note: 'Ich habe vor, zu...' = I plan to... Key B1 structure!
'-e' ending feminine words take -en in plural.
um=around + Welt=world. 'müssen' = must (modal verb).
fort=forward + Schritt=step. Literally 'steps forward'!
'-ung' nouns are ALWAYS feminine. Very reliable rule!
'unterzeichnen' = to sign. 'den' = accusative masculine.
'Lohn' = wage (hourly). 'Gehalt' = salary (monthly).
'im Büro' = at the office. 'ins Büro' = to the office.
'täglich' = daily. 'die Tablette' = tablet/pill.
From 'erkälten sich' = to catch a cold. '-ung' = always feminine!
Reise=journey + Pass=passport. 'meinen' = accusative masculine.
Uncountable. 'Handgepäck' = hand luggage (Hand+Gepäck)!

vocab.ex: Ich lade eine neue App herunter.
vocab.note: 'herunterladen' = to download (separable verb). English loanword.
'sicher' = safe/secure. 'muss...sein' = must be (modal).
'beeindruckend' = impressive. 'aus+stellen' = to exhibit.

vocab.ex: Die Wahl findet im Herbst statt.
vocab.note: 'stattfinden' = to take place (separable verb).
'alle Bürger' = all citizens (accusative plural).
Klima=climate + Wandel=change. Major topic in Germany!

vocab.ex: Die Banane ist gelb und süß.
vocab.note: Foreign word ending in -e, regularly feminine. Plural just adds -n.
French-style pronunciation. 'Orangensaft' = orange juice (compound word).
Usually used in plural (die Trauben) since grapes come in bunches.

vocab.ex: Erdbeeren schmecken im Sommer am besten.
vocab.note: Erd(e)=earth + Beere=berry. Compound word, very literal!

vocab.ex: Der Tee mit Zitrone ist lecker.
vocab.note: 'lecker' = tasty/delicious. Very common everyday word.
Uncountable like in English. 'Gemüse' = vegetables (also uncountable).
Always used in plural for a dish: 'Nudeln' even for one portion.
Uncountable. 'selten' = rarely. Many Germans say 'Fleischlos' for meatless.
'die Suppe' = soup. 'braucht' = needs (3rd person singular).
Knob(lauch) — historically related to 'cloves'. Uncountable noun.
Umlaut plural: Maus → Mäuse. Same word used for computer mouse!
Diminutive -chen ending makes nouns neuter. Same form in plural.
'springen' = to jump. Uses 'sein' in perfect tense (movement verb).

vocab.ex: Das Schwein lebt auf dem Bauernhof.
vocab.note: 'Bauernhof' = farm. 'Schweinefleisch' = pork (compound word).
Refers to chicken as food. The live animal is 'das Huhn'.

vocab.ex: Die Ente schwimmt auf dem See.
vocab.note: 'der See' = lake (masculine) vs 'die See' = sea (feminine). Same spelling!

vocab.ex: Das Baby schläft den ganzen Tag.
vocab.note: English loanword. Plural simply adds -s, unlike most German nouns.
Always plural — there's no German singular for 'one parent' as a single word.
Looks similar to English — easy cognate to remember!

vocab.ex: Ihr Sohn geht in die Schule.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural: Sohn → Söhne. 'Ihr' = her (possessive).

vocab.ex: Seine Tochter ist drei Jahre alt.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural: Tochter → Töchter. 'Seine' = his (possessive).
Means both 'man' and 'husband' depending on context — very common.
Means both 'woman' and 'wife'. Also the polite title 'Frau Schmidt' = Mrs. Schmidt.

vocab.ex: Die Blätter sind im Herbst orange.
vocab.note: Unlike most German adjectives, 'orange' and 'rosa' don't change their ending!
Also commonly said as 'lila' in everyday speech — both are correct.
Regular adjective — takes normal endings: 'ein braunes Haus'.
Like 'orange', 'rosa' never changes its ending — always stays 'rosa'.
Umlaut plural: Stuhl → Stühle. 'am Tisch' = an dem Tisch (contraction).

vocab.ex: Ich sitze gern auf dem Sofa.
vocab.note: International loanword, easy to remember. Plural just adds -s.

vocab.ex: Ich nehme jeden Morgen eine Dusche.
vocab.note: 'eine Dusche nehmen' = to take a shower (literally 'take a shower').
Essential travel phrase! French loanword, widely used and polite.
Umlaut plural: Schrank → Schränke. 'Kleiderschrank' = wardrobe specifically.

vocab.ex: Hast du einen Stift für mich?
vocab.note: General word for pen/pencil. 'Kugelschreiber' = ballpoint pen specifically.

vocab.ex: Ich schreibe alles in mein Heft.
vocab.note: Specifically a school exercise book, different from 'das Buch' (book).
Female: 'die Lehrerin'. The -in suffix for female professions is very common.
'Schüler' = school student. 'Student' is specifically for university.

vocab.ex: Der Mond ist heute Nacht voll.
vocab.note: 'voll' = full. 'Vollmond' = full moon (compound word).

vocab.ex: Die Sterne leuchten in der Nacht.
vocab.note: 'leuchten' = to shine/glow. Mercedes-Benz logo is called 'der Stern' too!
'wandern' = to hike. Germans love hiking — very cultural activity!

vocab.ex: Ich fahre im Sommer ans Meer.
vocab.note: 'ans' = an das (to the). 'Mittelmeer' = Mediterranean Sea (literally 'middle sea').

vocab.ex: Ich kaufe Blumen für meine Mutter.
vocab.note: 'Blumenladen' = flower shop. Giving flowers is a common gesture in Germany.
'Ich habe die Nase voll' = idiom meaning 'I'm fed up' (literally 'nose full')!
'Öffne' = open (imperative). Used by doctors and dentists commonly.
'tut weh' = hurts. 'weh tun' is a separable verb meaning 'to hurt'.

vocab.ex: Ich gehe zu Fuß zur Arbeit.
vocab.note: 'zu Fuß' = on foot (idiomatic phrase). Umlaut plural: Fuß → Füße.
Used for telling time: 'drei Uhr' = three o'clock. Also means the physical clock.
'Guten Tag!' = Good day! Common greeting any time before evening.
'Gute Nacht!' = Good night! Said when going to sleep, not as a greeting.

vocab.ex: Nächste Woche fahre ich nach Berlin.
vocab.note: 'Nächste Woche' = next week. 'Wochenende' = weekend (week+end).

vocab.ex: Im nächsten Monat habe ich Urlaub.
vocab.note: 'Urlaub' = vacation/holiday. Related to 'der Mond' (moon) historically.

vocab.ex: Ich lerne seit einem Jahr Deutsch.
vocab.note: 'seit' = since/for (with time). 'seit einem Jahr' = for one year (ongoing).
Always think plural — you rarely talk about just one sock!

vocab.ex: Im Winter trage ich eine Mütze.
vocab.note: For a soft hat/beanie. 'der Hut' is for a more structured hat.

vocab.ex: Ich habe meinen Schlüssel in der Tasche.
vocab.note: Also means 'pocket'! Context determines bag vs. pocket.

vocab.ex: Im Sommer trage ich eine Sonnenbrille.
vocab.note: Sonne=sun + Brille=glasses. Compound word, always singular form used (die Brille).

vocab.ex: Ich gehe zur Bank, um Geld abzuheben.
vocab.note: Also means 'bench' (die Bank, plural die Bänke)! Context matters.

vocab.ex: Ich gehe zur Post, um ein Paket zu schicken.
vocab.note: 'Paket' = package. 'schicken' = to send. Uncountable noun.
Super+Markt. 'Lebensmittel' = groceries (literally 'means of life').

vocab.ex: Wir gehen heute Abend ins Kino.
vocab.note: 'ins' = in das. 'heute Abend' = this evening. Common weekend activity.
Most German towns have a historic church as a landmark for directions.
Park+Platz (place/space). 'voll' = full, very common adjective.
Female form of 'der Lehrer'. 'geduldig' = patient.
French loanword. Note: no article needed for professions after 'sein'!
Female: 'die Anwältin'. 'vertreten' = to represent.

vocab.ex: Der Bauer arbeitet auf dem Feld.
vocab.note: 'das Feld' = field. Female: 'die Bäuerin'.
French-derived word. Female: 'die Friseurin'.
Long word but regular conjugation! Comes from 'das Foto' (photo).

vocab.ex: Er spielt seit drei Jahren Gitarre.
vocab.note: 'spielen' + instrument, no article needed: 'Gitarre spielen'.
For painting pictures. Different from 'streichen' (to paint walls).

vocab.ex: Ich fahre jeden Tag mit dem Fahrrad.
vocab.note: Fahr(en)+Rad. Germany has excellent cycling infrastructure!
Video+Spiel (game). English loanword combined with German noun.
Kredit+Karte. 'zahlen' = to pay. Cash is still very common in Germany!
Einkauf(s)+Wagen (cart/car). Often needs a coin deposit to unlock!
'Es gibt' = there is/are. Very useful shopping phrase.
'-ung' ending = always feminine. Cashiers often ask this.
'Rotwein' = red wine, 'Weißwein' = white wine. Germany has excellent wine regions!

vocab.ex: Ich nehme einen Salat als Vorspeise.
vocab.note: 'Vorspeise' = appetizer/starter. 'Hauptspeise' = main course.

vocab.ex: Im Sommer esse ich gerne Eis.
vocab.note: Same word for 'ice' and 'ice cream' — context clarifies which!

vocab.ex: Ich brauche einen Löffel für die Suppe.
vocab.note: Same form in singular and plural. 'die Gabel' = fork, 'das Messer' = knife.
Can mean 'friend' OR 'boyfriend' — context and tone clarify which!
Same ambiguity as 'Freund' — friend or girlfriend depending on context.
Strong word for romantic/deep love. 'mögen' is used for liking things casually.
'-ung' ending = feminine. Also used for international/business relations.
Opposite: 'krank' = sick. 'Gesundheit!' is also said after a sneeze!
'krankschreiben' = to sign off sick from work (common compound verb).
'-ung' ending = feminine. 'tut nicht weh' = doesn't hurt.
'-ung' = feminine. 'bestehen aus' = to consist of (common B1 verb).
'erneuerbar' = renewable. Major topic in German politics/society.
From 'möglich' (possible) + -keit suffix. Always feminine.

vocab.ex: Was ist der Unterschied zwischen den beiden?
vocab.note: 'zwischen' = between. 'unterscheiden' = to differentiate (related verb).

vocab.ex: Ich habe viel Erfahrung in diesem Bereich.
vocab.note: 'der Bereich' = field/area. Common in job interviews and CVs.
'erfolgreich' = successful. French loanword, easy to recognize.
zusammen=together + Arbeit=work. Long but logical compound word!
Essential business word — also used for doctor's appointments!
From 'lösen' (to solve). '-ung' ending = feminine, as always.

vocab.ex: Meine Aufgabe ist es, das Projekt zu leiten.
vocab.note: 'leiten' = to lead/manage. Also means 'homework' in school context!
Reflexive verb — needs 'sich/mich/dich' etc. 'Ich entspanne MICH'.

vocab.ex: Ich habe viel Stress bei der Arbeit.
vocab.note: English loanword, used identically to English. Uncountable.
'-ung' ending. Refers to overall diet/nutrition, not a weight-loss diet.

vocab.ex: Die Reise nach Italien war wunderschön.
vocab.note: 'Gute Reise!' = Have a good trip! Common farewell phrase.

vocab.ex: Wir haben eine günstige Unterkunft gefunden.
vocab.note: 'günstig' = affordable/good value. Umlaut plural: Unterkunft → Unterkünfte.
Sadly necessary vocabulary — even punctual Germany has train delays!

vocab.ex: Ich speichere meine Fotos in der Cloud.
vocab.note: English loanword used directly. 'speichern' = to save/store.
Short for 'Akkumulator'. 'der Akku ist leer' = the battery is dead.
Netz(work)+Werk. Used for both computer networks and social networks.
Germany has very strict and detailed recycling rules — multiple bins!

vocab.ex: Die globale Erwärmung betrifft uns alle.
vocab.note: 'globale Erwärmung' = global warming. 'betreffen' = to affect/concern.
Very common in modern German discourse around environment and business.

vocab.ex: Meiner Ansicht nach ist das richtig.
vocab.note: 'Meiner Ansicht nach' = In my view. Formal alternative to 'Meiner Meinung nach'.
Opposite: 'der Nachteil' = disadvantage. Vor=before/forward, Nach=after.

vocab.ex: Der Nachteil ist der hohe Preis.
vocab.note: Opposite of 'Vorteil'. 'hoch' (high) becomes 'hohe' before a noun.
'ausverkauft' = sold out. Used for concerts, theatre, opera performances.

vocab.ex: Sie interessiert sich für deutsche Literatur.
vocab.note: 'sich interessieren für' = to be interested in (reflexive + preposition).

vocab.ex: Die Information wurde der Öffentlichkeit zugänglich gemacht.
vocab.note: From 'öffentlich' (public) + -keit. 'zugänglich' = accessible.

vocab.ex: Soziale Gerechtigkeit ist ein wichtiges Thema.
vocab.note: From 'gerecht' (just/fair) + -keit. Common in political discourse.
'die Wirtschaft' = economy. International word, easy to recognize.
Bewusst(sein)=conscious + sein=being. 'wachsen' = to grow.

vocab.ex: Die kulturelle Identität spielt eine große Rolle.
vocab.note: 'eine Rolle spielen' = to play a role (idiomatic). Cognate, easy to spot.

vocab.ex: Aus dieser Perspektive sieht es anders aus.
vocab.note: 'aussehen' = to look/appear (separable verb: sieht...aus).
Also means 'pub/inn' in Austrian/Bavarian dialect — context matters!
'sicher' = safe/certain. International finance vocabulary, easy cognate.
'steigen' = to rise/climb (uses 'sein' in perfect tense).
From 'forschen' (to research). Germany invests heavily in scientific research.
'vorhersehbar' = predictable/foreseeable. Versatile word: tech, personal, societal development.

vocab.ex: Die Wissenschaft hat viele Fragen beantwortet.
vocab.note: Wissen(schaft)=knowledge. 'der Wissenschaftler' = scientist.
Long compound word! 'heftig' = intense/fierce. Can mean serious debate or conflict.
'ernst' = serious. Also means 'consistency' in a behavioral sense.
International word. 'das Handy' = mobile phone. 'klingeln' = to ring.
Same form in singular and plural. 'Schlüsselwort' = keyword (compound).
'Licht anmachen' = turn the light on. 'Licht ausmachen' = turn off.
'fegen' = to sweep. Same form singular/plural.
'Der Spiegel' is also Germany's most famous news magazine!
Confusing false friend — English 'handy' means convenient, but in German 'das Handy' = mobile phone! German invention of the word.
Masculine despite English neutral — same with many -er ending tech words.
From 'fernsehen' (to watch TV). The device (Fernseher) vs. the activity (fernsehen).
Arm(band)+Uhr. Long compound word — very German! 'kaputt' = broken (loanword from French).

vocab.ex: Ich lese die Zeitung jeden Morgen.
vocab.note: 'Zeit' = time. '-ung' = always feminine. 'Die Zeit' is also Germany's most famous weekly newspaper.
Double meaning: shop AND business/deal. 'Geschäftsmann' = businessman.
Umlaut plural: Strand → Strände. 'an den Strand' = to the beach (movement, accusative).
International word, easy to remember. Plural: Parks (foreign word, adds -s).
Flug(flight)+Hafen(harbour). Umlaut plural. 'Hafen' also means harbour/port — airports are 'flight harbours'!

vocab.ex: Der Zug fährt vom Bahnhof ab.
vocab.note: Bahn(railway/path)+Hof(courtyard). 'abfahren' = to depart (separable verb).
Uncountable. 'Wie ist das Wetter?' = What's the weather like? Essential small-talk topic!
'wolkig' = cloudy (adjective). 'bewölkt' = overcast. Common in weather descriptions.
Related to English 'wind'. 'Windig' = windy. 'Windmühle' = windmill (common in northern Germany).
Flug(flight)+Zeug(thing/tool) — literally 'flight thing'. 'starten' = to take off.

vocab.ex: Ich brauche ein Lineal zum Zeichnen.
vocab.note: From Latin 'linealis'. 'Zeichnen' = to draw. School supply essential.
Always singular in German (die Schere) even though it's plural in English. 'schneiden' = to cut.
drei(three)+Eck(corner). 'Seite' = side. Geometry vocabulary is easy in German!
Also means 'district/county' in German administrative divisions. 'rund' = round.
Italian loanword. Both plural forms accepted. Very popular in Germany.

vocab.ex: Zum Frühstück esse ich ein Brötchen.
vocab.note: Diminutive of 'Brot'. Germans often go to the bakery for fresh Brötchen on Sunday mornings.
From French 'soupe'. 'Hühnersuppe' = chicken soup. Very common in traditional German cooking.
'gießen' = to water. 'täglich' = daily. Many Germans love house plants.
Bad(bath)+Wanne(tub). 'Duschen' = to shower (verb), 'Baden' = to bathe (verb).
Same form singular/plural. 'Schlafzimmer' = bedroom, 'Wohnzimmer' = living room, 'Badezimmer' = bathroom.

vocab.ex: Die Heizung ist kaputt — es ist kalt!
vocab.note: From 'heizen' (to heat). '-ung' = always feminine. Heating is very important in Germany!
From French 'régal'. 'stehen' used for upright objects on shelves. IKEA (Swedish) has sold many German Regale!

vocab.ex: Der Pinguin lebt in der Antarktis.
vocab.note: International word. Popular in German children's books and TV.
Hand(hand)+Schuh(shoe) — 'hand shoes'! Almost always plural. Singular: 'der Handschuh' (one glove).
Almost always plural. Singular: 'der Schuh'. 'Schuhgeschäft' = shoe shop.
Umlaut plural: Hut → Hüte. Different from 'die Mütze' (soft hat/beanie). 'Hutmacher' = hat-maker.
'kauen' = to chew. Same form in singular/plural. 'Knochenbruch' = fracture (bone break).

vocab.ex: Ich putze zweimal täglich die Zähne.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural: Zahn → Zähne. 'Zahnarzt' = dentist. 'Zähneputzen' = brushing teeth.

vocab.ex: Wir besuchen das Museum am Wochenende.
vocab.note: From Greek via Latin. Unusual plural: Museum → Museen. Germany has over 6,000 museums!

vocab.ex: Das Fußballspiel findet im Stadion statt.
vocab.note: Greek loanword. Plural: Stadien. 'stattfinden' = to take place (separable).
Rat(council)+Haus(house). Marriages in Germany happen at the Standesamt (registry office), often in the Rathaus.
Same form singular/plural. 'ins Theater gehen' = go to the theatre (accusative after 'in' = movement).
Famous bridges: Köln (Cologne), Dresden, Hamburg. 'über die Brücke gehen' = walk over the bridge.
Umlaut plural. 'Fernsehturm' = TV tower (famous in Berlin). 'hoch' = high/tall.
From Latin 'ampulla'. 'bei Rot warten' = wait at red. Famous: Ampelmännchen (East German traffic light man icon).
Note the ß (Eszett). 'Hauptstraße' = main street. Swiss German writes 'Strasse' (no ß).
Krank(sick)+Schwester(sister). Male nurse: 'der Krankenpfleger'. Long compound — very German!
Same form singular/plural. Feminine: 'die Mechanikerin'. Greek loanword via French.
n-declension: den Architekten, dem Architekten. Feminine: 'die Architektin'. 'entwerfen' = to design.
From French 'clavier' (keyboard). 'Klavier spielen' — no article. Germany has a strong classical music tradition.

vocab.ex: Wir gehen am Samstag ins Schwimmbad.
vocab.note: Schwimm(swim)+Bad(bath). Umlaut plural. Most German towns have a public 'Freibad' (outdoor pool) or 'Hallenbad' (indoor pool).
'Brettspiel' = board game, 'Computerspiel' = video game. 'Spaß machen' = to be fun.

vocab.ex: Ich mache Fotos mit meiner Kamera.
vocab.note: From camera obscura (Latin). Plural: -s (foreign word). 'Foto machen' = take a photo.

vocab.ex: Wir schlafen im Zelt beim Camping.
vocab.note: 'Zelten' = to camp (in a tent). 'Campingplatz' = campsite. Camping is very popular in Germany.
Land+Karte. 'Stadtplan' = city map. Modern: 'Google Maps' is used, but maps are still sold.
Same form singular/plural. 'Handgepäck' = hand luggage. 'Koffer packen' = to pack a suitcase.
French loanword. Plural: -s. 'übernachten' = to stay overnight. 'Zimmer buchen' = to book a room.
Kauf(buy)+Haus. Umlaut plural. Famous: Kaufhof, Karstadt (now Galeria). 'KaDeWe' in Berlin is Europe's largest department store.
'nehmen' = to take. 'täglich' = daily. 'Apotheke' = pharmacy — prescription needed for many drugs in Germany.
Double meaning! Medical prescription AND cooking recipe. Context required. 'Kochrezept' = recipe (to clarify).

vocab.ex: Regelmäßige Bewegung ist wichtig für die Gesundheit.
vocab.note: From 'bewegen' (to move). 'regelmäßig' = regular. '-ung' always feminine.

vocab.ex: Ich brauche ein Pflaster für die Wunde.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. Also means 'cobblestone/pavement'. 'Wunde' = wound. 'Verbandkasten' = first aid kit.

vocab.ex: In Deutschland ist Recycling sehr wichtig.
vocab.note: Germany has one of the world's best recycling systems — Pfand (deposit) system for bottles, strict waste separation.

vocab.ex: Wir genießen die frische Luft in der Natur.
vocab.note: Uncountable. 'Naturschutz' = nature conservation. Germans deeply value Naturschutz — large protected areas across the country.

vocab.ex: Die Temperatur ist heute sehr hoch.
vocab.note: International word. 'Grad Celsius' = degrees Celsius (Germany uses Celsius, not Fahrenheit).

vocab.ex: Ohne Internet kann ich nicht arbeiten.
vocab.note: 'Das Netz' = the net (informal). 'WLAN' = WiFi (Wireless Local Area Network — pronounced [veː.ɛl.ʔaː.ˈʔɛn]).
'schicken' = to send. Feminine in standard German, neuter in colloquial ('das Email'). Written with hyphen.
Bild(picture)+Schirm(screen/shield). 'dunkel' = dark. 'Helligkeit' = brightness.
Same form singular/plural. From 'drucken' (to print). 'Tinte' = ink, 'Toner' = toner.
Also means 'a few': 'ein paar' (lowercase) = a few. 'heiraten' = to marry.
'glücklich' = happy. 'heiraten' = to get married, 'verheiratet sein' = to be married.
Same singular/plural. Feminine: 'die Rentnerin'. 'die Rente' = pension. Germany has generous pension system.
Feminine: 'die Nachbarin'. Plural: Nachbarn (n-type). 'Nachbarschaft' = neighbourhood.
Steck(plug)+Dose(tin/socket). German sockets are type F (Schuko) — need adaptor in UK/US.
'hinaufgehen' = to go up. 'Treppenhaus' = stairwell. 'im ersten Stock' = on the first floor (UK), second floor (US).
Umlaut plural: Garten → Gärten. 'Schrebergarten' = allotment garden — very popular German institution.
Wasser+Hahn(cock/tap). Umlaut plural. 'zudrehen' = to turn off (separable). Tap water in Germany is safe to drink.

vocab.ex: Meine Haut ist trocken im Winter.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural. 'trocken' = dry. 'Hautcreme' = skin cream. Important in medicine and beauty.

vocab.ex: Er trainiert seine Muskeln im Fitnessstudio.
vocab.note: From Latin 'musculus' (little mouse — shape resemblance!). 'Fitnessstudio' = gym.
'schlagen' = to beat. 'Herzschlag' = heartbeat. 'von Herzen' = from the heart (expression).
'rauchen' = to smoke. 'schaden' + dative = to damage. Smoking has declined significantly in Germany.

vocab.ex: Wir machen einen Spaziergang im Wald.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural. 'Schwarzwald' = Black Forest (famous tourist destination). 'Waldsterben' = forest dieback (environmental issue).
SAME WORD, different gender, different meaning! Der See = lake. Die See = sea/ocean. This is one of German's most confusing word pairs!

vocab.ex: Die Kühe grasen auf dem Feld.
vocab.note: 'grasen' = to graze. Also means 'field' in football: 'Spielfeld'. 'Feld' appears in many compounds.
Unusual plural: Datum → Daten (also means 'data'!). 'Welches Datum?' = What's the date? Context determines singular/plural meaning.

vocab.ex: Wir fahren im Sommer in Urlaub.
vocab.note: 'in Urlaub fahren' = go on holiday. 'Urlaubsreise' = holiday trip. Germans have 20-30 paid vacation days per year by law!
'Herzlichen Glückwunsch zum Geburtstag!' = Happy Birthday (formal). Casual: 'Alles Gute zum Geburtstag!'
'Wie spät ist es?' = What time is it? (common). 'Wie ist die Uhrzeit?' = more formal. Uhr(clock)+Zeit(time).

vocab.ex: Meiner Meinung nach ist das richtig.
vocab.note: 'meiner Meinung nach' = in my opinion. 'Was ist Ihre Meinung?' = What is your opinion? Key phrase for discussions.

vocab.ex: Sie studiert an der Universität Berlin.
vocab.note: 'studieren' = to study (at university). 'lernen' = to learn/study (at school level). This distinction matters in German!
From 'prüfen' (to test/check). 'Abitur' = German A-levels (university entrance exam). 'durch die Prüfung fallen' = fail the exam.
Haus(home)+Aufgabe(task). Almost always plural in everyday use: 'Hausaufgaben machen' = do homework.

vocab.ex: Kann ich bitte die Weinkarte haben?
vocab.note: Wein+Karte. Germany is famous for Riesling wines. 'Speisekarte' = food menu.

vocab.ex: Als Vorspeise nehme ich die Suppe.
vocab.note: Vor(before)+Speise(dish). 'Hauptgericht' = main course. 'Nachspeise/Dessert' = dessert.
Haupt(main)+Gericht(dish/court). 'ausgezeichnet' = excellent. Key restaurant vocabulary.
Trink(drink)+Geld(money). In Germany, tipping ~10% is polite but not mandatory like in the US. Round up and say 'stimmt so' (keep the change).

vocab.ex: Ich schreibe eine Bewerbung für die Stelle.
vocab.note: From 'sich bewerben' (to apply). 'Bewerbungsunterlagen' = application documents. 'Lebenslauf' = CV.
Leben+Lauf (life+run). Umlaut plural. German CVs include photo, date of birth — different from US/UK norms.

vocab.ex: Ich bewerbe mich um eine Stelle als Ingenieur.
vocab.note: 'sich bewerben um' = to apply for. 'Arbeitsstelle' = workplace. Different from 'der Job' (more casual).
From 'abteilen' (to divide). 'Personalabteilung' = HR department. '-ung' always feminine.
n-declension: den Kollegen. Feminine: 'die Kollegin'. From Latin 'collega' (co-elected).

vocab.ex: Ich mache viele Überstunden diese Woche.
vocab.note: Über(over)+Stunde(hour). Almost always plural in use. 'Überstunden machen' = work overtime.

vocab.ex: Ich arbeite zwei Tage im Homeoffice.
vocab.note: English loanword now firmly established in German. Became ubiquitous after 2020. 'im Homeoffice arbeiten'.

vocab.ex: In Deutschland sind die Steuern hoch.
vocab.note: 'Steuererklärung' = tax return. 'Mehrwertsteuer (MwSt.)' = VAT. Germany has 19% VAT and high income taxes.
Health insurance is mandatory in Germany (Pflicht = obligation). 'Haftpflicht' = liability insurance.

vocab.ex: Ich schaue die Nachrichten um 20 Uhr.
vocab.note: 'Tagesschau' (20:00) is Germany's most watched news show. Also means 'message': 'eine Nachricht schicken'.

vocab.ex: Die moderne Gesellschaft hat viele Herausforderungen.
vocab.note: Double meaning: (1) society/community, (2) company/firm (GmbH). 'Herausforderung' = challenge.

vocab.ex: Wir leben in einer starken Gemeinschaft.
vocab.note: Gemein(common/shared)+schaft(ship). 'Europäische Gemeinschaft' = European Community.
Solar+Energie. Uncountable. Germany is among the world's leading solar power producers.

vocab.ex: Windkraft ist in Norddeutschland sehr wichtig.
vocab.note: Wind+Kraft(power). Uncountable. Northern Germany has thousands of wind turbines ('Windräder').

vocab.ex: Bitte wirf den Müll in die richtige Tonne.
vocab.note: Uncountable. 'Mülltrennung' = waste separation (mandatory in Germany — blue/yellow/brown/green bins!).
'Umweltschutz' = environmental protection. 'Naturschutzgebiet' = nature reserve. From 'schützen' (to protect).
Umlaut plural. 'planen' = to plan. 'Stadtplan' = city map. 'Businessplan' = business plan (English loanword).
International word. 'Ich habe ein Problem' = I have a problem. 'kein Problem!' = no problem!

vocab.ex: Die Digitalisierung ist eine große Herausforderung.
vocab.note: Heraus(out)+Forderung(demand). Long but very useful. 'eine Herausforderung meistern' = master a challenge.

vocab.ex: Er hält einen Vortrag über Klimawandel.
vocab.note: Vor(before)+Trag(carry/present). Umlaut plural. 'einen Vortrag halten' = give a lecture/talk.

vocab.ex: Ich lese gerade einen Roman von Thomas Mann.
vocab.note: From French 'roman'. Famous German novelists: Goethe, Schiller, Kafka, Mann, Hesse, Grass.

vocab.ex: Wir gehen am Freitag ins Konzert.
vocab.note: From Italian 'concerto'. 'ins Konzert gehen' = go to a concert. Germany has excellent classical music scene.

vocab.ex: Ihr Buch war ein großer Erfolg.
vocab.note: Er(prefix)+Folg(follow → result). 'erfolgreich' = successful. 'viel Erfolg!' = good luck! (literally: much success!).
Also adjective: 'fest' = firm/solid. 'Oktoberfest' (Munich), 'Karneval' (Köln), 'Weihnachtsmarkt' = Christmas market.
International word from Latin. 'ein Experiment durchführen' = conduct an experiment.

vocab.ex: Die Technologie entwickelt sich sehr schnell.
vocab.note: From Greek. 'sich entwickeln' = to develop. 'Spitzentechnologie' = cutting-edge technology.
Germany's 'Digitalisierung' of public services has been slow — this is a widely discussed political topic.

vocab.ex: Das Wirtschaftswachstum war dieses Jahr gering.
vocab.note: Uncountable. 'Wirtschaftswachstum' = economic growth. 'gering' = small/modest. Key economics term.

vocab.ex: Die Automobilindustrie ist wichtig für Deutschland.
vocab.note: From French 'industrie'. Germany's 'Mittelstand' (SMEs) and car industry are pillars of its economy.
Umlaut plural. 'Wochenmarkt' = weekly market (very common in German towns). 'Marktplatz' = market square.
Uncountable. 'Außenhandel' = foreign trade. 'handeln' = to trade/act/negotiate. Germany is the world's 3rd largest exporter.
Frei(free)+heit(suffix). 'Grundrecht' = basic right. Germany's Basic Law (Grundgesetz) protects many Freiheiten.

vocab.ex: Kreativität ist in der Kunst sehr wichtig.
vocab.note: From 'kreativ' (creative). '-ität' always feminine. International suffix recognizable from many languages.
Verbal noun from 'wissen' (to know). 'Wissen ist Macht' = Knowledge is power (Bacon). Neuter (all verbal nouns are).

vocab.ex: Was ist die Bedeutung dieses Wortes?
vocab.note: From 'bedeuten' (to mean). Double usage: meaning of a word AND importance of something. 'bedeutend' = significant.

vocab.ex: Der Einfluss von Social Media ist groß.
vocab.note: Ein(in)+Fluss(flow). Umlaut plural. 'beeinflussen' = to influence (verb). 'einflussreich' = influential.
Zusammen(together)+Hang(hang). Umlaut plural. 'im Zusammenhang mit' = in connection with. Essential academic word.

vocab.ex: Mein Neffe ist fünf Jahre alt.
vocab.note: n-declension: den Neffen. Feminine: 'die Nichte'. From Latin 'nepos'.
From Latin 'neptia'. 'Onkel' = uncle. 'Tante' = aunt.

vocab.ex: Der Enkel besucht die Großeltern jede Woche.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. Feminine: 'die Enkelin'. 'Enkelkind' = grandchild.

vocab.ex: Er macht eine Therapie nach dem Unfall.
vocab.note: From Greek. 'Psychotherapie' = psychotherapy. Mental health awareness growing in Germany.

vocab.ex: Ich gehe dreimal pro Woche ins Fitnessstudio.
vocab.note: English 'fitness' + Italian 'studio'. 'ins Fitnessstudio gehen' = go to the gym.

vocab.ex: Das Medikament bekomme ich in der Apotheke.
vocab.note: From Greek 'apotheke' (storehouse). In Germany, pharmacists (Apotheker) are highly trained — you go to them for advice.
From Greek 'pan' (all) + 'demos' (people). 'Epidemie' = epidemic (smaller scale).

vocab.ex: Sie hat ihren Abschluss mit Auszeichnung gemacht.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural. 'Schulabschluss' = school leaving cert. 'Universitätsabschluss' = university degree.
Germany has a tripartite school system: Hauptschule / Realschule / Gymnasium. Gymnasium leads to Abitur (university).

vocab.ex: Ich muss einen Aufsatz über Klimawandel schreiben.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural. 'Auf'(upon)+Satz(sentence/set). Schul-Aufsatz = school essay. 'Erörterung' = argumentative essay.

vocab.ex: In Deutschland gibt es selten Erdbeben.
vocab.note: Erd(earth)+Beben(shaking). 'selten' = rarely. Same form singular/plural.
'Flutkatastrophe' = flood disaster. The 2021 Ahr valley flood was Germany's worst in decades. 'Hochwasser' = high water/flooding.

vocab.ex: Die Sahara ist die größte Wüste der Welt.
vocab.note: From 'wüst' (waste/barren). 'trocken' = dry. Germany has no deserts but students study world geography.

vocab.ex: Die Gletscher in den Alpen schmelzen.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. 'schmelzen' = to melt. Glacier retreat is a visible impact of climate change in Alps.

vocab.ex: Die Vergangenheit kann man nicht ändern.
vocab.note: Also a grammar term: 'Vergangenheit' = past tense. 'Gegenwart' = present, 'Zukunft' = future (the 3 time dimensions).
'ungewiss' = uncertain. Also grammar: 'Zukunft' = future tense. 'zukunftsorientiert' = future-oriented.
'Lebe im Hier und Jetzt' = Live in the here and now. Also grammar: 'Präsens/Gegenwart' = present tense.
International word. 'Gegenargument' = counter-argument. 'argumentieren' = to argue (with reasoning, not fighting).

vocab.ex: Die politische Debatte war sehr interessant.
vocab.note: From French 'débat'. 'debattieren' = to debate. 'eine Debatte führen' = hold a debate.
From 'einigen' (to agree/unify). 'erzielen' = to achieve. 'Kompromiss' = compromise.
From Greek 'thema'. Plural: Themen. 'Gesprächsthema' = topic of conversation. 'thematisieren' = to address a topic.
From Latin 'aspectus'. 'berücksichtigen' = to take into consideration. Key academic vocabulary.
Collective noun, uncountable. Individual vegetables: 'Brokkoli', 'Zucchini', 'Aubergine'. 'Gemüsehändler' = greengrocer.
'würzen' = to season. 'Salz und Pfeffer' = salt and pepper. 'scharf' = spicy.

vocab.ex: Zum Frühstück esse ich ein Croissant.
vocab.note: French loanword. Keeps French pronunciation mostly. German bakeries ('Bäckerei') sell many international pastries alongside traditional German ones.

vocab.ex: Das Restaurant liegt in der Innenstadt.
vocab.note: Innen(inner)+Stadt. Umlaut plural. Also: 'Stadtzentrum', 'Stadtmitte'. German city centres ('Altstadt' = old town) often have medieval architecture.
Same form singular/plural. From 'bauen' (to build). 'Gebäude' = general term; 'Haus' more specific/smaller.
Bau(construction)+Stelle(place). Germans joke that Berlin is permanently a 'eine einzige große Baustelle' (one big construction site).

vocab.ex: Von hier hat man eine wunderschöne Aussicht.
vocab.note: Also means 'prospects': 'gute Berufsaussichten' = good career prospects. 'Aus'(out)+Sicht(view).

vocab.ex: Der Radweg ist für Fahrräder reserviert.
vocab.note: Rad(wheel/bike)+Weg(path). Germany has extensive cycling infrastructure, especially in cities like Amsterdam-style Hamburg and Berlin.

vocab.ex: Ich parke mein Auto im Parkhaus.
vocab.note: Park+Haus. Umlaut plural. Different from 'Tiefgarage' (underground car park).
Tank+Stelle. 'tanken' = to fill up with fuel. Germany has the famous Autobahn — but even it has speed limits in many sections!
English loanword. Plural: -s. 'Was sind deine Hobbys?' = What are your hobbies? Popular: Wandern, Lesen, Kochen, Radfahren.
English loanword. Also called 'Geduldsspiel' (patience game). Very popular in Germany, especially during winter.

vocab.ex: Wandern in den Alpen ist wunderschön.
vocab.note: Verbal noun. 'wandern' = to hike. Germans are passionate hikers — 'Wanderweg' (hiking trail) network covers thousands of km.
Verbal noun from 'kochen'. 'Lieblingshoby' = favourite hobby. 'Kochkurs' = cooking class. Very popular in Germany.

vocab.ex: Der Bergsteiger hat den Gipfel erreicht.
vocab.note: Berg+Steiger(climber). Same form singular/plural. Feminine: 'die Bergsteigerin'. 'Gipfel' = summit/peak.

vocab.ex: Die Ambivalenz dieser Entscheidung ist offensichtlich.
vocab.note: From Latin 'ambi' (both) + 'valere' (to be strong). 'ambivalent' = adjective. Key in psychology and critical writing.

vocab.ex: Transparenz in der Politik ist wichtig.
vocab.note: From Latin 'trans' (through) + 'parere' (appear). 'transparent' = adjective. Important in governance and business.

vocab.ex: Die Effizienz des Prozesses muss verbessert werden.
vocab.note: From French 'efficience'. 'effizient' = efficient. 'Effektivität' ≠ 'Effizienz': effectiveness vs. efficiency.

vocab.ex: Der Text hat eine gute Kohärenz.
vocab.note: From Latin 'cohaerere' (to stick together). Academic writing must have 'Kohärenz'. 'kohärent' = coherent/consistent.

vocab.ex: Innovation ist der Schlüssel zum wirtschaftlichen Erfolg.
vocab.note: From Latin 'innovare' (to renew). 'Innovationskraft' = innovative power. Germany prides itself on 'Made in Germany' quality.

vocab.ex: Nachhaltigkeit ist ein zentrales Konzept der heutigen Zeit.
vocab.note: From 'nachhaltig' (sustainable). Uncountable. Originally a forestry term from 18th century Germany — take only what can regrow.

vocab.ex: Die Wechselwirkung zwischen Politik und Wirtschaft ist komplex.
vocab.note: Wechsel(exchange)+Wirkung(effect). Complex term for mutual influence between systems. Key in physics and social science.

vocab.ex: Klare Zielsetzungen sind wichtig für den Erfolg.
vocab.note: Ziel+Setzung. 'SMART-Ziele' (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) are used in German management.

vocab.ex: Deutschland hat viel Potenzial im Bereich Erneuerbare Energien.
vocab.note: From Latin 'potentia'. 'potentiell' = potential (adjective). 'sein Potenzial entfalten' = realise one's potential.

vocab.ex: Das Grundgesetz ist die Verfassung Deutschlands.
vocab.note: Also: 'in guter/schlechter Verfassung' = in good/bad condition. 'Grundgesetz' (Basic Law, 1949) is Germany's constitution.
From Greek 'demos' (people) + 'kratos' (rule). Germany's post-WWII democracy is considered a model of 'streitbare Demokratie' (militant democracy).

vocab.ex: Deutschland setzt auf Diplomatie statt Konfrontation.
vocab.note: From French 'diplomatie'. 'Diplomat' = diplomat. Germany plays a major role in European diplomacy.

vocab.ex: Die deutsche Bürokratie ist bekannt für ihre Gründlichkeit.
vocab.note: From French 'bureau' + Greek 'kratos'. Germany is famously thorough in its administrative procedures — a cultural trait sometimes seen as both strength and weakness.

vocab.ex: Die Integration von Migranten ist eine gesellschaftliche Aufgabe.
vocab.note: From Latin 'integrare'. Key political concept in Germany's immigration debate. 'Integrationskurs' = mandatory language/civics course for migrants.

vocab.ex: Solidarität ist ein Grundprinzip der Europäischen Union.
vocab.note: From French 'solidarité'. 'Solidaritätszuschlag' = solidarity surcharge (tax to rebuild East Germany after reunification 1990).
BIP for short. One of the longest compound words in everyday German economics. Germany has one of the world's largest GDPs.

vocab.ex: Deutschland ist eine der größten Exportnationen der Welt.
vocab.note: From Latin 'exportare'. 'Exportüberschuss' = trade surplus. Germany runs one of the world's largest trade surpluses.

vocab.ex: Die Lieferkette wurde durch die Pandemie gestört.
vocab.note: Liefer(delivery)+Kette(chain). 'gestört' = disrupted. 'Lieferkettengesetz' (2023) = German supply chain due diligence law.

vocab.ex: Berlin ist bekannt für seine Startup-Szene.
vocab.note: English loanword. Berlin is Germany's startup capital (nicknamed 'Silicon Allee'). 'Gründer' = founder. 'Gründergeist' = entrepreneurial spirit.
Also 'KI' (abbreviation). 'Branche' = sector/industry. Germany is investing heavily in KI research.

vocab.ex: Die Gentechnik in der Medizin bietet neue Möglichkeiten.
vocab.note: Gen+Technik. Uncountable. Germany is cautious about GM food ('Gentechnik in Lebensmitteln') but supports medical applications.

vocab.ex: Die Quantenphysik wurde von deutschen Wissenschaftlern maßgeblich entwickelt.
vocab.note: Quantum physics was largely developed in Germany: Planck, Heisenberg, Einstein. 'maßgeblich' = significantly.

vocab.ex: Deutschland hat eine starke Pharmaindustrie (Bayer, BASF).
vocab.note: Pharma+Industrie. Uncountable. Germany's pharmaceutical industry (Bayer, BASF, Merck) is globally significant.
Kultur+Erbe(heritage/inheritance). Germany has 52 UNESCO World Heritage Sites — one of the highest numbers globally.

vocab.ex: Die Aufklärung im 18. Jahrhundert veränderte Europa.
vocab.note: Double meaning: (1) the Enlightenment movement, (2) to inform/clarify. Kant's famous essay 'Was ist Aufklärung?' (1784). German Enlightenment thinkers: Kant, Lessing, Herder.

vocab.ex: Jeder Mensch hat eine eigene Weltanschauung.
vocab.note: Welt+Anschauung(view/perspective). A distinctly German concept borrowed into English as 'Weltanschauung'. Related: 'Lebensphilosophie'.

vocab.ex: Das Feuilleton der FAZ ist bekannt für seine Qualität.
vocab.note: French loanword. The Feuilleton (culture/arts pages) of major German newspapers (FAZ, SZ, Zeit) are taken very seriously — cultural commentary is part of civic life.

vocab.ex: Bach und Beethoven sind Meister der deutschen Klassik.
vocab.note: German classical music tradition is unparalleled: Bach, Handel, Haydn, Mozart (Austrian), Beethoven, Schubert, Brahms, Wagner.

vocab.ex: Die Wahrnehmung von Risiken hängt von der Erfahrung ab.
vocab.note: Wahr(true)+Nehmung(taking). Philosophical/psychological term. 'wahrnehmen' = to perceive/be aware of.

vocab.ex: Ein Paradigmenwechsel findet in der Energiepolitik statt.
vocab.note: From Greek. 'Paradigmenwechsel' = paradigm shift (concept from Thomas Kuhn). Common in academic and political discourse.

vocab.ex: Die Komplexität des Systems ist eine Herausforderung.
vocab.note: From 'komplex' (complex). Uncountable. 'Komplexitätsreduktion' = simplification. Important in management and systems theory.

vocab.ex: Umweltschutz hat für uns höchste Priorität.
vocab.note: From Latin 'prior' (first). 'Prioritäten setzen' = to set priorities. 'vorrangig' = priority (adjective).

vocab.ex: Die wirtschaftliche Prognose für 2025 ist positiv.
vocab.note: From Greek 'prognosis' (foreknowledge). 'prognostizieren' = to predict. Used in economics, weather, medicine.

vocab.ex: Die Legitimität der Entscheidung wurde in Frage gestellt.
vocab.note: From Latin 'legitimus' (lawful). 'legitim' = legitimate. Key concept in political science and law. Max Weber defined 3 types of legitimacy.

vocab.ex: Die kulturelle Vielfalt in Deutschland ist groß.
vocab.note: Viel(much)+Falt(fold). 'kulturelle Vielfalt' = cultural diversity. 'Biodiversität' = biodiversity. Synonyms: 'Diversität', 'Pluralismus'.

vocab.ex: In Deutschland werden Entscheidungen oft im Konsens getroffen.
vocab.note: From Latin 'consentire' (to agree). German political culture values consensus ('Kompromisskultur'). Coalition governments require consensus.

vocab.ex: Die Zuwanderung ist ein wichtiges politisches Thema.
vocab.note: 'Einwanderung' also common (= immigration). 'Auswanderung' = emigration. Germany received over a million refugees in 2015-16.
'laut + dative' = according to. 'Statistisches Bundesamt' = Federal Statistics Office (Destatis). Data-driven governance is important in Germany.

vocab.ex: Die Hypothese wurde durch Experimente bestätigt.
vocab.note: From Greek 'hypothesis'. 'bestätigen' = to confirm. 'Forschungshypothese' = research hypothesis. Academic writing requires clear hypotheses.

vocab.ex: Die Methodik der Studie war einwandfrei.
vocab.note: 'einwandfrei' = flawless. 'Methode' (specific method) vs 'Methodik' (overall approach/methodology). Key for academic papers.

vocab.ex: Die Auswertung der Daten dauerte eine Woche.
vocab.note: From 'auswerten' (to evaluate/analyse data). 'Datenauswertung' = data analysis. Common in research and business contexts.

vocab.ex: Der Algorithmus von Google beeinflusst, was wir sehen.
vocab.note: From Arabic mathematician Al-Khwarizmi. 'algorithmisch' = algorithmic. Central to computer science and AI discussions.

vocab.ex: Die Rendite der Investition war höher als erwartet.
vocab.note: From Italian 'rendita'. Key financial term. 'Aktienrendite' = share return. German savers historically prefer low-risk investments.
From Latin 'capacitas'. 'Auslastung' = utilisation rate. 'Kapazitätserweiterung' = capacity expansion.

vocab.ex: Deutschland ist in der globalen Wertschöpfungskette gut positioniert.
vocab.note: Wert(value)+Schöpfung(creation)+Kette(chain). Michael Porter's value chain concept is widely used in German business education.

vocab.ex: Die Liquidität des Unternehmens muss gesichert werden.
vocab.note: From Latin 'liquidus'. Key financial term. 'liquide sein' = to be liquid/solvent. 'Liquiditätsengpass' = liquidity bottleneck.

vocab.ex: Das kulturelle Erbe muss bewahrt werden.
vocab.note: 'Erbe' as neuter = heritage. 'der Erbe' (masc.) = heir. 'bewahren' = to preserve. Important in cultural policy discussions.

vocab.ex: Die Rezension des Romans in der FAZ war sehr positiv.
vocab.note: From Latin 'recensio'. 'rezensieren' = to review. German newspapers take literary and cultural reviews very seriously — a distinctive feature of German public discourse.

vocab.ex: Der Regisseur hat einen Oscar gewonnen.
vocab.note: From French 'régisseur'. Feminine: 'die Regisseurin'. Famous German directors: Werner Herzog, Wim Wenders, Fatih Akın.

vocab.ex: Das Manuskript wurde nach Jahren endlich veröffentlicht.
vocab.note: From Latin 'manu scriptum' (written by hand). 'veröffentlichen' = to publish. Gutenberg (German) invented the printing press — printing heritage is valued in Germany.

vocab.ex: Das Paradoxon der Toleranz ist bekannt.
vocab.note: 'Paradox der Toleranz' = Popper's Paradox of Tolerance (1945) — tolerating intolerance undermines tolerance. Two plural forms accepted.

vocab.ex: Die Prämissen des Arguments müssen überprüft werden.
vocab.note: From Latin 'praemissa'. 'überprüfen' = to verify. Key term in logic, philosophy, and academic argumentation.

vocab.ex: Das Konzept der Nachhaltigkeit ist komplex.
vocab.note: From Latin 'concipere'. 'konzeptuell' = conceptual. 'ein Konzept entwickeln' = develop a concept. Also means 'draft/plan' in business.

vocab.ex: Der politische Diskurs in Deutschland ist vielfältig.
vocab.note: From Latin 'discursus'. Jürgen Habermas (German philosopher) developed influential 'discourse ethics' theory. Common in academic writing.

vocab.ex: Die Dynamik des Arbeitsmarktes hat sich verändert.
vocab.note: From Greek 'dynamis' (power). 'dynamisch' = dynamic. Used in physics, economics, sociology. 'Systemdynamik' = systems dynamics.

vocab.ex: Die Menschenrechte sind universell und unveräußerlich.
vocab.note: Mensch(human)+Rechte(rights). Always plural. 'unveräußerlich' = inalienable. Germany's Basic Law (Grundgesetz) places human rights first.

vocab.ex: Die neuen Regelungen treten ab 2025 in Kraft.
vocab.note: From 'regeln' (to regulate). Broader than 'Gesetz' (law) — includes administrative rules, agreements. Germany is known for detailed Regelungen.

vocab.ex: Innere Sicherheit ist ein wichtiges Staatsziel.
vocab.note: Triple meaning: safety (physical), security (national), certainty (epistemic). 'Datensicherheit' = data security. 'Arbeitssicherheit' = workplace safety.
'grundlegend' = fundamentally. Automation is a central challenge for Germany's manufacturing-heavy economy.
English/Latin loanword now firmly in German business vocabulary. 'disruptiv' = disruptive. 'Startups' often aim to create 'Disruption'.
'Arbeitsproduktivität' = labour productivity. Germany is among the world's most productive economies despite relatively short working hours.

vocab.ex: Der Artikel muss das Peer-Review-Verfahren bestehen.
vocab.note: English loanword in academic German. 'peer-reviewed' = 'begutachtet'. Academic publishing requires Peer-Review for quality assurance.

vocab.ex: Das Konzeptpapier wurde dem Ministerium vorgelegt.
vocab.note: Konzept+Papier. 'vorlegen' = to submit/present. German bureaucracy and research require many different paper types: 'Positionspapier', 'Arbeitspapier'.
Wagner coined this term for his operas — works combining music, drama, poetry, visual arts into one unified whole. Now used more broadly for any ambitious interdisciplinary creation.

vocab.ex: Für die Hausarbeit muss ich viel Sekundärliteratur lesen.
vocab.note: Sekundär+Literatur. 'Primärliteratur' = primary sources (original texts). 'Hausarbeit' = term paper. Essential academic vocabulary.

vocab.ex: Resilienz ist eine wichtige Eigenschaft in der modernen Arbeitswelt.
vocab.note: From Latin 'resilire' (to spring back). 'resilient' = adjective. Became very common in German after COVID-19 — 'Resilienz' appeared everywhere in management and politics.

vocab.ex: Die Konvergenz der europäischen Volkswirtschaften ist ein Ziel der EU.
vocab.note: From Latin 'convergere'. 'konvergieren' = to converge. Used in economics, physics, and mathematics. Opposite: 'Divergenz'.

vocab.ex: Die Proliferation von Informationen im Internet ist enorm.
vocab.note: From Latin 'proles' (offspring). 'Nuklearproliferation' = nuclear proliferation. Also used for spread of information, technologies.

vocab.ex: Deutschland setzt auf Multilateralismus in der Außenpolitik.
vocab.note: Multi+lateral+ismus. Germany strongly supports multilateral institutions (UN, EU, WTO) as a matter of foreign policy doctrine.

Grund(basic)+Gesetz(law). Germany's constitution since 1949. Deliberately called 'Grundgesetz' not 'Verfassung' — originally seen as provisional until reunification. Reunification (1990) didn't change the name.

vocab.ex: Rechtsstaatlichkeit ist ein Grundprinzip der EU.
vocab.note: Rechtsstaat(constitutional state)+lichkeit. The German 'Rechtsstaat' concept influenced global constitutional theory. Rechtsstaatlichkeit is a core EU value — currently contested.
From Latin 'coniunctura'. 'Konjunkturpaket' = economic stimulus package. 'Hochkonjunktur' = boom, 'Konjunkturflaute' = slowdown.

vocab.ex: Deregulierung kann Wachstum fördern, aber auch Risiken erhöhen.
vocab.note: de(removal)+Regulierung(regulation). Economic policy debate: more or less regulation? Germany tends toward 'Ordnungspolitik' (regulatory framework) rather than pure deregulation.

vocab.ex: Ich putze meine Zähne mit der Zahnbürste.
vocab.note: Zahn(tooth)+Bürste(brush). 'Zahnpasta' = toothpaste. Daily routine vocabulary.
'Handseife' = hand soap. 'Duschgel' = shower gel (more common than bar soap nowadays).
Same form singular/plural. 'Kopfkissen' = head pillow (specific). 'weich' = soft.

vocab.ex: Im Winter brauche ich eine warme Decke.
vocab.note: Double meaning: blanket OR ceiling. Context determines. 'Bettdecke' = duvet (specific).
From Greek 'bibliothēkē'. German university libraries (Unibibliothek) are often very quiet and well-organised.

vocab.ex: Mein Auto ist in der Werkstatt.
vocab.note: Werk(work)+Statt(place). Umlaut plural. For car repairs: 'Autowerkstatt' or 'Kfz-Werkstatt'.
Same form singular/plural. 'dicht' = dense/thick. Common in autumn in Germany, especially near rivers.

vocab.ex: Das Gewitter war sehr laut letzte Nacht.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. 'Donner' = thunder, 'Blitz' = lightning. Summer thunderstorms are common in Germany.

vocab.ex: Wir nehmen ein Taxi zum Flughafen.
vocab.note: International word. Plural: -s. 'Taxistand' = taxi rank. Apps like Uber and FreeNow also operate in Germany.

vocab.ex: E-Roller sind in vielen Städten beliebt.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. 'E-Roller' = e-scooter. Very popular in German cities for short trips since ~2019.
Verbal noun. Traditional German pub activity, slightly different from American 10-pin bowling (uses 9 pins traditionally).
Always plural for the game. Singular 'die Karte' = card/menu/ticket (context-dependent). 'Skat' = popular German card game.
Male: 'der Sekretär'. Modern term: 'Assistentin' or 'Büroassistentin' often preferred today.

vocab.ex: Der Pilot landet das Flugzeug sicher.
vocab.note: n-declension: den Piloten. Feminine: 'die Pilotin'. 'sicher landen' = to land safely.
From French 'paquet'. 'Paketdienst' = parcel service (DHL is the main German one). 'ankommen' (sep.) = to arrive.
Kasse(cash register)+Zettel(slip/note). Also called 'Quittung' or 'Bon'. Important to keep for returns.
'eine Präsentation halten' = to give a presentation. International word, common loanword in business German.

vocab.ex: Wir haben heute eine Telefonkonferenz mit dem Team.
vocab.note: Telefon+Konferenz. 'Videokonferenz' = video call — more common since 2020. 'Zoom-Meeting' also widely used.

vocab.ex: Bitte füllen Sie dieses Formular aus.
vocab.note: 'ausfüllen' (sep.) = to fill out. German bureaucracy involves many Formulare — patience is needed!
'Personalausweis' = national ID card (mandatory for German citizens). 'Reisepass' = passport (separate document).

vocab.ex: Im Wald sieht man oft Rehe.
vocab.note: Specifically roe deer (smaller species). 'Hirsch' = larger deer/stag. Common wildlife in German forests.

vocab.ex: Der Fuchs lebt auch in der Stadt.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural. Urban foxes are increasingly common in German cities. 'schlau wie ein Fuchs' = clever as a fox (idiom).
Same form singular/plural. From 'wecken' (to wake someone). 'den Wecker stellen' = to set the alarm.
Same form singular/plural. From 'malen' (to paint). More formal than 'Bild' (picture).
From Latin 'sculptura'. International word. Public art (Skulpturen) common in German city squares.
Short for 'Laboratorium'. From Latin 'laborare'. Germany has many leading research labs (Max Planck Institutes).

vocab.ex: Der Euro ist die Währung in Deutschland.
vocab.note: From 'währen' (to last/endure). 'Fremdwährung' = foreign currency. Germany uses the Euro since 2002.
From Latin 'actio'. 'Aktienmarkt' = stock market. Germans traditionally invest less in stocks than Americans.

vocab.ex: Die Braut trägt ein weißes Kleid.
vocab.note: Umlaut plural. 'der Bräutigam' = groom. 'Hochzeit' = wedding.
From 'Braut' + 'Gomo' (man, archaic). 'Altar' = altar (church wedding). Often shortened informally to 'Brautmann' colloquially.
From 'heucheln' (to feign/pretend). 'Heuchler' = hypocrite. Common in political and social commentary.

vocab.ex: Die Empörung über den Skandal war groß.
vocab.note: From 'sich empören' (to be outraged). '-ung' always feminine. Strong emotional reaction, common in news media.
Meinung(opinion)+Freiheit(freedom). Protected explicitly in Article 5 of the German Grundgesetz (constitution).

vocab.ex: Der Konflikt zwischen den beiden Ländern dauert an.
vocab.note: From Latin 'conflictus'. 'andauern' (sep.) = to continue/persist. 'Konfliktlösung' = conflict resolution.

vocab.ex: Die Europäische Zentralbank legt die Zinssätze fest.
vocab.note: Zentral(central)+Bank. 'EZB' = Europäische Zentralbank (ECB). 'Zinssatz' = interest rate. Headquartered in Frankfurt.

vocab.ex: Die Wirtschaft befindet sich in einer Rezession.
vocab.note: From Latin 'recessio'. 'sich in einer Rezession befinden' = to be in a recession. Key economics vocabulary.

vocab.ex: Die Neurowissenschaft erklärt, wie das Gehirn funktioniert.
vocab.note: Neuro+Wissenschaft. Growing field in German research institutions. 'Gehirn' = brain. 'funktionieren' = to function.
From Latin 'manifestus'. 'Aufsehen sorgen' = to cause a sensation. Common in art and political history (e.g. Bauhaus Manifest).

vocab.ex: Pluralität der Meinungen ist gesund für die Demokratie.
vocab.note: From Latin 'pluralitas'. Related to 'Vielfalt' but more formal/philosophical. Common in political theory texts.

vocab.ex: Die Verarmung bestimmter Bevölkerungsgruppen ist besorgniserregend.
vocab.note: From 'verarmen' (to become poor). 'besorgniserregend' = worrying/alarming. Sociological/economic vocabulary.

vocab.ex: Die Standortwahl ist entscheidend für den Unternehmenserfolg.
vocab.note: Standort(location)+Wahl(choice). Business decision-making term. 'entscheidend' = decisive/crucial.

vocab.ex: Deutschland will die CO2-Emissionen drastisch reduzieren.
vocab.note: From Latin 'emittere'. 'Treibhausgasemissionen' = greenhouse gas emissions. Central to climate policy debates.

vocab.ex: Die Biotechnologie eröffnet neue medizinische Möglichkeiten.
vocab.note: Bio+Technologie. Growing sector in Germany, especially after mRNA vaccine development (BioNTech).

vocab.ex: Der Grundsatz der Verhältnismäßigkeit ist zentral im deutschen Recht.
vocab.note: Verhältnis(relation/proportion)+mäßig+keit. Long but important legal/constitutional term — limits on state action must be proportionate.

vocab.ex: Die staatliche Überwachung wird kontrovers diskutiert.
vocab.note: From 'überwachen' (to monitor). Germans are historically very sensitive about surveillance (due to Stasi/Gestapo history) — strong data protection culture.
Regen(rain)+Schirm(screen). 'mitnehmen' (sep.) = to take along. Essential item in Germany's often rainy climate.
From French 'batterie'. Different from 'Akku' (rechargeable). 'leer' = empty/dead.

vocab.ex: Der Brunnen auf dem Marktplatz ist sehr alt.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. 'Marktplatz' = market square. Common feature in German town centres.

vocab.ex: Das Schloss Neuschwanstein ist sehr berühmt.
vocab.note: Triple meaning: castle/palace AND lock (door mechanism). Umlaut plural. Neuschwanstein is Germany's most iconic castle.

vocab.ex: Mein Vater geht jeden Sonntag angeln.
vocab.note: Verbal noun. 'angeln gehen' = to go fishing. Popular weekend hobby, requires a Fischereischein (fishing license) in Germany.

vocab.ex: Mein Sohn hat einen Hamster als Haustier.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. 'Haustier' = pet. Popular first pet for children in Germany.

vocab.ex: Ich arbeite diese Woche in der Nachtschicht.
vocab.note: 'Nachtschicht' = night shift. 'Schichtarbeit' = shift work. Common in healthcare, manufacturing, retail.

vocab.ex: Am Feiertag haben die Geschäfte geschlossen.
vocab.note: Feier(celebration)+Tag(day). Germany has different public holidays per Bundesland (federal state) — varies regionally!

vocab.ex: Der öffentliche Rundfunk ist in Deutschland sehr wichtig.
vocab.note: Rund(round)+Funk(radio signal). 'öffentlich-rechtlicher Rundfunk' = public broadcasting (ARD, ZDF) — funded by mandatory fee (Rundfunkbeitrag).

vocab.ex: Die Presse berichtet ausführlich über das Ereignis.
vocab.note: Uncountable (collective). 'Pressefreiheit' = freedom of the press — constitutionally protected in Germany.

vocab.ex: Ich schneide die rote Paprika in Streifen.
vocab.note: From Hungarian via Serbian. 'Streifen' = strips. Common in salads and stir-fries.

vocab.ex: Die Gurke ist sehr erfrischend im Sommer.
vocab.note: 'erfrischend' = refreshing. 'Salzgurke' = pickled cucumber — a German deli staple.
Same form singular/plural. From Greek 'orchestra'. Germany has many world-class orchestras (Berliner Philharmoniker).

vocab.ex: Der Sänger hat eine wunderschöne Stimme.
vocab.note: Same form singular/plural. Feminine: 'die Sängerin'. 'Stimme' = voice.

vocab.ex: Persönliche Autonomie ist ein wichtiger Wert.
vocab.note: From Greek 'autonomia'. 'autonom' = autonomous. Philosophical and political vocabulary.

vocab.ex: Die Heterogenität der Gesellschaft nimmt zu.
vocab.note: From Greek. 'zunehmen' (sep.) = to increase. Sociological and academic vocabulary, opposite of 'Homogenität'.

vocab.ex: Die politische Repräsentation von Minderheiten ist ein wichtiges Thema.
vocab.note: From Latin 'representare'. 'Minderheit' = minority. Political science vocabulary.

vocab.ex: Die politische Polarisierung hat in den letzten Jahren zugenommen.
vocab.note: From 'polarisieren' (to polarise). Common in discussions about social media's effect on political discourse.

vocab.ex: Die digitale Infrastruktur muss ausgebaut werden.
vocab.note: From Latin 'infra' (below) + 'structura'. 'ausbauen' (sep.) = to expand. Major German policy topic (roads, rail, broadband).

vocab.ex: Der Strukturwandel in der Kohleindustrie betrifft viele Arbeitsplätze.
vocab.note: Struktur+Wandel. 'Kohleindustrie' = coal industry. Major topic in Germany's transition away from coal (Kohleausstieg).

vocab.ex: Die Theorie muss durch Empirie gestützt werden.
vocab.note: From Greek 'empeiria' (experience). 'stützen' = to support. 'empirisch' = empirical. Academic research methodology term.

vocab.ex: Die Validität der Studie wurde in Frage gestellt.
vocab.note: From Latin 'validus'. 'in Frage stellen' = to question. Key research methodology term alongside 'Reliabilität' (reliability).

vocab.ex: Die Avantgarde der 1920er-Jahre prägte die moderne Kunst.
vocab.note: French loanword. 'prägen' = to shape/define. German Bauhaus and Expressionism were major avant-garde movements.

vocab.ex: Die Rezeption des Romans war gemischt.
vocab.note: From Latin 'receptio'. 'gemischt' = mixed. Different from hotel 'Rezeption' (reception desk) — context determines meaning.

vocab.ex: Der Verlust der Biodiversität ist alarmierend.
vocab.note: Bio+Diversität. 'Verlust' = loss. 'alarmierend' = alarming. Key environmental science term.

vocab.ex: Die Energiewende ist ein zentrales Projekt der deutschen Klimapolitik.
vocab.note: Energie+Wende(turn/transition). Distinctly German policy term (since ~2000) for shift from fossil/nuclear to renewable energy.

freq.note: Most important verb. Highly irregular: ich bin, du bist, er ist, wir sind.
2nd most important. Also forms perfect tense: Ich habe gegessen.
Always capitalised in German! Unlike English 'I' — no, wait, same rule!
Goes AFTER the verb usually. 'Ich gehe nicht' = I am not going.
Feminine AND plural 'the'. Also relative pronoun 'who/which'!
Masculine 'the'. Changes case: der/den/dem/des.
Neuter 'the'. Also means 'that/which' as relative pronoun.
Does NOT change word order! 'und' is a coordinating conjunction.
Location: in + dative. Movement: in + accusative. Key distinction!
Always + dative. 'von mir' = from me. Used instead of genitive often!
Always + dative. 'mit dem Bus' = by bus. Very versatile!
Always + dative. 'zum' = zu dem. 'zur' = zu der.
Also used for masculine nouns: 'Der Hund — er ist groß.'
Lowercase 'sie' = she OR they. Capital 'Sie' = formal you!
Used for neuter nouns AND weather/time: 'Es regnet. Es ist 5 Uhr.'

freq.ex: Das Buch liegt auf dem Tisch.
freq.note: Location: auf + dative. Movement: auf + accusative.

freq.ex: Das Bild hängt an der Wand.
freq.note: For vertical surfaces and edges. 'am' = an dem.
Goes after the subject usually. 'Ich auch' = Me too!

freq.ex: Ich will, aber ich kann nicht.
freq.note: Coordinating conjunction — no word order change after 'aber'!
'noch nicht' = not yet. 'noch einmal' = once more.
'so...wie' = as...as. 'so groß wie' = as big as.

freq.ex: Erst esse ich, dann gehe ich.
freq.note: Time connector. When at start of sentence, verb comes before subject!

freq.ex: Wenn ich Zeit habe, komme ich.
freq.note: Subordinating: verb goes to END. 'wenn' = when (repeated) OR if.

freq.ex: Ich lerne, weil es wichtig ist.
freq.note: Subordinating: verb goes to END! 'weil es wichtig IST' not 'ist es'!
Subordinating conjunction. Verb goes to END: 'dass er KOMMT'.
Modal verb: conjugated verb + infinitive at END. 'Ich kann...sprechen.'
'muss' (singular), 'müssen' (plural). Strong obligation!
'will' ≠ future tense in German! It means 'want'. 'Ich will' = I want!
Permission. 'Darf ich?' = May I? Very polite question!
External obligation (told by someone else). 'Ich soll' = I am supposed to.
'mögen' = to like. 'möchten' = would like (polite form of mögen).
Uses 'sein' in perfect! 'Ich bin gegangen.' Movement verbs use sein!
'Woher' = from where. Uses 'sein' in perfect tense.
'Was machst du?' = What are you doing? Extremely common verb!
'Gibt es' = there is/are. 'Es gibt' = very important phrase!
Strong verb: sehen → siehst/sieht. 'bis bald' = see you soon!
'wissen' = know facts. 'kennen' = know people/places. Different!
'Ich denke, dass' introduces a subordinate clause. Verb to end!
'hier' = here. 'da' = there. 'dort' = over there (further away).
'jetzt' = now. 'gerade' = right now/just. Similar but slightly different!
'immer' = always. 'manchmal' = sometimes. 'nie' = never.
'sehr' = very. 'zu' = too (negative). 'Es ist zu heiß' = too hot.
'mehr' = more. 'weniger' = less. 'am meisten' = most.
'schon' = already. Also softens: 'Das wird schon klappen' = It'll be fine.
'nur' = only/just. 'Nur ein bisschen' = just a little bit.
'mal' softens commands. 'Komm mal her' = Come here (friendly, not harsh)!
'doch' contradicts a negative. 'Kommst du nicht?' 'Doch!' = Yes I am!
Adds curiosity/depth. 'Was machst du eigentlich?' = What do you actually do?
'vielleicht' = maybe. 'wahrscheinlich' = probably. 'sicher' = certainly.
'natürlich' = of course. 'selbstverständlich' = goes without saying (B2).
'der Mensch' (m), irregular: den/dem/des Menschen in all cases except nominative!
'das Jahr' (n). 'Dieses' = this (neuter demonstrative).
'die Zeit' (f). 'keine Zeit haben' = to not have time (very common).
'der Weg' (m). Also means 'route'. 'unterwegs' = on the way (adverb).
'die Frage' (f). 'fragen' = to ask (the verb form).
'die Hand' (f). 'zur Hand haben' = to have on hand (idiom).

freq.ex: Das ist nur ein Teil des Problems.
freq.note: 'der Teil' (m), though sometimes 'das Teil' for a physical piece/component.
'die Welt' (f). 'Weltkrieg' = world war. 'überall in der Welt' = all over the world.
'das Leben' (n), from the verb 'leben' (to live). Nominalized infinitive.
Double use: 'simple' (adjective) or 'simply/just' (adverb): 'Ich will einfach schlafen.'
Very common filler/agreement word too: 'Genau!' = Exactly!

freq.ex: Das ist sehr wichtig für mich.
freq.note: Regular adjective. 'Wichtigkeit' = importance (noun form).
Opposite: 'unmöglich' = impossible. Simply add 'un-' prefix.
'Alles klar?' = Everything clear/OK? Common casual check-in phrase.
As adverb means 'quite/rather'. 'ganz schön' = quite/pretty (intensifier).
Comparative: länger. 'lange' (adverb) = for a long time: 'Ich warte lange.'
Opposite of 'lang'. 'kurz vor' = shortly before (time expression).
Used for age of people AND things. Comparative: älter (umlaut).
'neu' takes adjective endings: 'ein neues Handy' (neuter accusative).
Comparative: jünger (umlaut). Opposite of 'alt'.
Comparative of 'gut' (good) — irregular, not 'guter'!
'Mir geht es schlecht' = I'm not feeling well. Common health expression.
Opposite: 'falsch' = wrong. Also used as intensifier: 'richtig gut' = really good.
Opposite of 'richtig'. 'leider' = unfortunately (common softener).
'Ist dieser Platz frei?' = Is this seat free? Essential public transport phrase.
'fertig sein' = to be done/ready. Very commonly used in daily life.
'Das ist nicht nötig' = That's not necessary. Polite way to decline help.
'bereit sein, zu...' = to be ready to... Common with infinitive clauses.

freq.ex: Plötzlich fing es an zu regnen.
freq.note: 'anfangen' = to begin (separable: fing...an, past tense).
Expresses relief. 'Endlich!' alone is a common exclamation.

freq.ex: Während des Urlaubs habe ich viel gelesen.
freq.note: As preposition takes GENITIVE: 'während DES Urlaubs'. As conjunction, sends verb to end.
Takes accusative. 'Bis bald!' = See you soon! Very common farewell.

freq.ex: Ich wohne seit drei Jahren hier.
freq.note: Always + dative. Used with present tense for ongoing duration — unlike English!
Always + accusative. 'ohne...zu' = without doing... (infinitive clause).

freq.ex: Wir spielen gegen die andere Mannschaft.
freq.note: Always + accusative. Also used for approximate time: 'gegen 5 Uhr' = around 5.
Always + accusative. Also expresses cause: 'durch Zufall' = by chance.
Location: dative. Movement: accusative. 'über' + topic = 'about' (always accusative then).

freq.ex: Die Katze schläft unter dem Tisch.
freq.note: Location: dative. Movement: accusative. Also means 'among': 'unter Freunden'.

freq.ex: Der Stuhl steht neben dem Tisch.
freq.note: Location: dative. Movement: accusative. Two-way preposition like 'in/auf/an'.

freq.ex: Berlin liegt zwischen Hamburg und München.
freq.note: Location: dative. Movement: accusative. 'zwischen...und' = between...and.
Declines like 'der': jeder/jede/jedes depending on gender.
Plural form. 'alles' (neuter singular) = everything — different word!
Used for an indefinite subset. 'manche Leute' = some people.
Similar to 'manche' but slightly more neutral/factual in tone.
Plural quantifier. Singular 'viel' = much: 'viel Geld' (uncountable).
Opposite of 'viele'. 'ein wenig' (without -e) = a little bit — different meaning!
'nichts' never changes form. 'Macht nichts!' = No worries/never mind!
Opposite: 'niemand' = no one. Both decline slightly in dative: jemandem.
Negation pronoun. Don't double-negate: 'Niemand war nicht da' is wrong!
Also used as intensifier: 'etwas müde' = a bit tired.
Regular weak verb. Without 'zu' before infinitive in negative: 'Du brauchst nicht zu kommen' (colloquially drops 'zu' too).
Double meaning: 'to find' (locate) AND 'to find/think' (opinion): 'Ich finde, dass...'
'Ich glaube' = I think/believe (very common hedge in conversation).
Uses 'sein' in perfect tense (like movement verbs): 'Ich bin geblieben.'

freq.ex: Das Auto steht vor dem Haus.
freq.note: Also used for objects' position, not just people standing. Uses 'haben' typically.

freq.ex: Das Buch liegt auf dem Tisch.
freq.note: For flat/horizontal position. 'Berlin liegt in Deutschland' = Berlin is located in Germany.
'sich setzen' (reflexive) = to sit down (the action). 'sitzen' = to BE sitting (state).

freq.ex: Kannst du mir das Buch bringen?
freq.note: Irregular: brachte (past), gebracht (participle). Strong verb pattern.
Strong verb: du nimmst, er nimmt (vowel change e→i). Very common.
Vowel change: du hältst, er hält. Means both 'to hold' and 'to stop' (vehicles).
Takes DATIVE: 'mir folgen' = follow me. Uses 'sein' in perfect tense.
Inseparable 'er-' prefix. Used for both physical arrival and goal achievement.
Strong verb: verlor, verloren. Inseparable 'ver-' prefix.
Strong verb: gewann, gewonnen. Opposite of 'verlieren'.
Essential learner phrase: 'Was bedeutet...?' = What does...mean?
Strong verb: vergaß, vergessen. Inseparable 'ver-' prefix, no 'ge-' added.
Reflexive for 'to remember': 'sich erinnern an' + accusative.
+ infinitive with 'zu': 'Ich versuche, früh aufzustehen.'
'Ich habe es geschafft!' = I made it/managed it! Very common celebratory phrase.
Uses 'sein' in perfect (like movement/change verbs): 'ist passiert', not 'hat'.
Reflexive for personal interest: 'sich interessieren für' = to be interested in.
Inseparable 'be-' prefix, regular weak verb otherwise.

freq.ex: Ich schlage vor, dass wir gehen.
freq.note: Separable verb! 'schlage...vor'. Key for B1 conversations and meetings.

freq.ex: Wir haben einen neuen Ort entdeckt.
freq.note: Inseparable 'ent-' prefix. 'der Ort' = place/location.
Strong verb: vermied, vermieden. Inseparable 'ver-' prefix.

freq.ex: Es war schwer, dennoch hat sie es geschafft.
freq.note: Synonym of 'trotzdem', slightly more formal/written register. Causes inversion too.

freq.ex: Ich bin müde, deshalb gehe ich schlafen.
freq.note: Causes verb-subject inversion: 'deshalb GEHE ich' (verb second position rule).
Takes dative: 'außer MIR' = except me. Also means 'apart from/besides'.
Very versatile intensifier. 'nichts Besonderes' = nothing special (noun use).
Frequency adverb. Scale: nie→selten→manchmal→oft→immer (never→always).
From 'meist' (most). Very common in describing habits and routines.
Often has slightly negative connotation (constant complaint/repetition).
Strong intensifier, often used with 'müssen' for emphasis.
Near-negative. 'kaum jemand' = hardly anyone.
Not related to English 'fast' (speed)! That's 'schnell' in German.
'dauern' = to take/last (time). Synonym: 'etwa'.
Confirms surprising truth. 'wahr' = true. Stronger than 'eigentlich'.
From 'hoffen' (to hope) + -lich. Sentence-initial adverb, very common.
Common softener for bad news or declining invitations politely.
Indicates an action in progress right now. Also means 'straight' (direction).
Strong urgency. 'so' + 'fort' (away/forth) historically.
Comparative of 'spät' (late). 'Bis später!' = See you later!
Comparative of 'früh' (early), but also means 'in the past' generally.
über+all. Opposite: 'nirgends/nirgendwo' = nowhere.
Opposite of 'überall'. Less common than 'nirgends' but equally correct.
Opposite: 'drinnen' = inside. Common in weather/activity descriptions.
Opposite of 'draußen'. 'bei Regen' = in case of rain (common weather phrase).

freq.ex: Das Buch liegt oben auf dem Schrank.
freq.note: Opposite: 'unten' = down/below. Static location, not direction.
Opposite of 'oben'. For direction use 'nach oben/unten' instead.
neben+an. 'der Nachbar' = neighbor (related word).
Indefinite pronoun. Accusative: jemanden. Dative: jemandem. Opposite: niemand (nobody).
Negative indefinite pronoun. Accusative: niemanden. Dative: niemandem. Pair with 'jemand'.
Indeclinable indefinite pronoun. Also used adverbially: 'etwas lauter' = somewhat louder. Opposite: nichts.
Indeclinable negative pronoun. 'Nichts ist unmöglich' = Nothing is impossible. 'nichts zu danken' = don't mention it.
Pronominal adjective for two things. Declines like plural adjective. 'Beides' = both things (neutral).
Always plural, declines like adjective without article. 'mehrere Male' = several times. Between 'einige' (some) and 'viele' (many).

freq.ex: Einige Studenten haben die Prüfung nicht bestanden.
freq.note: Plural indefinite: some (but not many). 'einige Male' = a few times. Sing. 'einiges' = some things (neuter).

freq.ex: Welches Buch liest du? Haben Sie noch welche?
freq.note: Interrogative pronoun (which?) AND indefinite (some). 'Hast du Zucker?' 'Ja, ich habe welchen' = some.
Demonstrative: the same. Written as one word with definite article attached: derselbe/dieselbe/dasselbe. Not to be confused with 'der gleiche' (an identical but different one).
Reciprocal pronoun, indeclinable. More formal than 'sich': 'miteinander' (together), 'voneinander' (from each other), 'füreinander' (for each other).

freq.ex: Das habe ich bisher nicht gewusst.
freq.note: 'Bis' (until) + 'her' (here). Points from past to present moment. 'bisher nicht' = not yet/not so far.
Slightly more formal than 'schon'. 'Bereits jetzt' = even now/already now. Often interchangeable with 'schon'.

freq.ex: Ich habe das noch nicht gemacht.
freq.note: 'Noch' (still) + 'nicht' (not) = not yet. Opposite: 'schon' (already). 'Noch nicht fertig' = not done yet.
Simple time adverb. 'so bald wie möglich' = as soon as possible (ASAP). 'baldigst' = as soon as possible (formal).

freq.ex: Ich komme gleich! Das ist gleich wichtig.
freq.note: As adverb: immediately/in a moment. As adjective: same/equal. 'Gleichheit' = equality. 'gleichzeitig' = simultaneous.

freq.ex: Plötzlich fing es an zu regnen.
freq.note: Common narrative adverb. 'plötzlich' at start of sentence is very common in storytelling. Related: 'auf einmal' (all of a sudden).
'Langsamer' = slower (comparative). Opposite: schnell. 'langsam aber sicher' = slowly but surely (common phrase).
Both adverb and adjective. 'so schnell wie möglich' = ASAP. 'schnellstmöglich' = as quickly as possible (formal).
Very common modal adverb. When in position 1: 'Leider KANN ich nicht kommen' (verb inversion). Polite way to decline.
'Hoffe' (hope) + ntlich. Modal adverb expressing desire. Often at sentence start. 'Das wäre zu hoffen' = that would be hoped.
Emphasises that something really happened/is true. Stronger than 'eigentlich'. 'Tatsache' = fact. 'in der Tat' = indeed (formal).
'Grundsatz' (principle) + lich. Useful for qualified agreement: 'In principle yes, but...'. Common in discussions and negotiations.

freq.en: in any case / at any rate
freq.ex: Jedenfalls war es eine gute Erfahrung.
freq.note: 'jede' (every) + 'falls' (case/fall). 'Auf jeden Fall' = definitely. Used to assert something regardless of other considerations.

freq.ex: Schließlich haben wir eine Lösung gefunden.
freq.note: 'Schluss' (end/close) + lich. Two uses: (1) temporal = finally, (2) reasoning = after all/when all is said. Context determines.

freq.ex: Zunächst müssen wir das Problem verstehen.
freq.note: 'Zu' + 'nächst' (next/nearest). Formal alternative to 'erstens' or 'zuerst'. Common in formal writing and presentations.
From 'anschließen' (to follow/connect). More formal than 'danach'. Common in texts describing sequence of events.

freq.ex: Ansonsten habe ich nichts zu sagen.
freq.note: 'An' + 'sonsten' (otherwise). Used to introduce an alternative situation or wrap up: 'Otherwise, everything is fine.'
'In' + 'zwischen' (between). Indicates something happened in the interval. 'mittlerweile' is a near synonym.
Similar to 'inzwischen'. Suggests gradual change over time. 'mittlerweile ist es klar' = by now it's clear.

freq.ex: Ich bin zwar müde, aber ich komme trotzdem.
freq.note: Always in pair 'zwar...aber' (admittedly...but). Concedes a point before contradicting it. 'Das stimmt zwar, aber...'

freq.ex: Ich komme nicht, ich bin nämlich krank.
freq.note: Explains reason/clarification. Different from English 'namely' — used more like 'you see' or 'because you see'. NEVER at start.
Concessive particle. Can mean (1) however/though (contradictive), or (2) certainly/to be sure (emphatic). Context determines.

freq.en: on the other hand / in contrast
freq.ex: Er ist ruhig, sie hingegen ist laut.
freq.note: Literary/formal alternative to 'dagegen' (on the other hand). Marks clear contrast between two things.

freq.ex: Es war schwer, dennoch hat er es geschafft.
freq.note: Formal equivalent of 'trotzdem'. 'Er schaffte es dennoch' = he managed it nonetheless. Common in written German.

freq.ex: Es gibt keine Lösung, somit müssen wir abbrechen.
freq.note: 'So' + 'mit'. Logical consequence connector. More formal than 'deshalb'. Common in academic and legal texts.

freq.ex: Es regnete stark, infolgedessen blieben viele zu Hause.
